finding.summary: The track exhibits a pronounced low-frequency emphasis with a dull high-end response, likely sounding muddy or lacking clarity.
finding.probable_cause: The synth patch has an inherent low-frequency bias, and no EQ or processing is applied to balance the spectrum.
finding.confidence_reason: Only three 1/3-octave bands are available, but they consistently show a 12 dB drop from 100 Hz to 4 kHz, indicating a strong low-end tilt. The low silence fraction (0.05) ensures levels are reliable.

evidence_refs.description: 1/3-octave band levels: 100 Hz at -18 dB, 1 kHz at -24 dB, 4 kHz at -30 dB. A 12 dB drop over three octaves confirms low-frequency dominance.

proposal.reason: No EQ or other processing exists in the FX chain to address the tonal imbalance. Adding an EQ would be the typical remedy, but the isolated track data cannot justify a specific cut or boost without hearing the full mix. The track level and headroom are within safe ranges, so no urgent gain change is indicated.
